ucs.title: [LLM] Confucius weaponized Office doc: WINWORD remote-template fetch chained to Equation Editor (EQNEDT32) child execution
ucs.description: Hunts Confucius's signature delivery chain — a spearphishing RTF/DOCX that uses remote template injection (T1221) to pull a second-stage payload, followed by the Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE) exploiting CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0802 to spawn a scripting host or RAT loader (warzone, Sneepy, Hornbill droppers).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","eqnedt32.exe")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("eqnedt32.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("winword.exe","eqnedt32.exe") AND All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.app | rename All_Traffic.src as host | fields host All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.app] | where (parent_process_name="winword.exe" AND process_name="eqnedt32.exe") OR (parent_process_name="eqnedt32.exe" AND process_name IN ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe")) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime host user parent_process_name process_name process All_Traffic.dest_ip
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 1d;
let suspChildren = dynamic(["eqnedt32.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","certutil.exe"]);
let officeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","eqnedt32.exe"]);
let procs = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
| where FileName in~ (suspChildren)
| project ProcTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ReportId;
let net = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (officeParents)
| where RemotePort in (80, 443)
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetInitiator=InitiatingProcessFileName;
procs
| join kind=inner net on DeviceId
| where abs(datetime_diff('second', ProcTime, NetTime)) <= 120
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "winword.exe" and FileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe")
   or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe" and FileName in~ (dynamic(["mshta.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe"])))
| project ProcTime, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
ucs.rationale: Template injection followed by EQNEDT32 spawning a scripting host is THE Confucius/Patchwork delivery fingerprint observed since 2017 in campaigns against Pakistani/South Asian government and military targets (TrendMicro 2018/2021, Uptycs 2021). Equation Editor abuse (CVE-2017-11882/0802) was the group's preferred client-side exploit long after most actors moved on, and chaining it to a remote-tem

ucs.title: [LLM] Confucius mshta-driven HTA loader establishing scheduled task persistence to cloud-staged C2
ucs.description: Hunts the post-exploit Confucius pattern where mshta.exe is launched (often by EQNEDT32 or wscript) to retrieve an HTA from an attacker-controlled site or legitimate cloud service, then registers a scheduled task pointing at a staged binary in %APPDATA% or %PUBLIC% that beacons to web infrastructure (T1583.006) used to deliver Hornbill / Sunbird / Warzone implants.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="mshta.exe" AND (Processes.process="*http*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process as mshta_cmd parent_process_name as mshta_parent | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND Processes.process="*/create*" AND (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process="*.exe*" OR Processes.process="*.dll*") by host Processes.process Processes.user | rename Processes.process as schtask_cmd Processes.user as schtask_user | fields host schtask_cmd schtask_user] | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.app="mshta.exe" OR All_Traffic.app="powershell.exe") AND All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.app | rename All_Traffic.src as host All_Traffic.app as net_app | fields host net_app All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_ip] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime host user mshta_parent mshta_cmd schtask_cmd net_app All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_ip
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 1d;
let mshta = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName =~ "mshta.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://",".hta")
| project MshtaTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, MshtaParent=InitiatingProcessFileName, MshtaCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let sched = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "/create"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("\\AppData\\","\\Public\\","\\ProgramData\\","\\Temp\\",".exe",".dll",".vbs",".js")
| project SchedTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, SchedCmd=ProcessCommandLine, SchedUser=AccountName;
let net = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","wscript.exe")
| where RemotePort in (80, 443)
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetApp=InitiatingProcessFileName;
mshta
| join kind=inner sched on DeviceId
| where datetime_diff('minute', SchedTime, MshtaTime) between (-30 .. 30)
| join kind=inner net on DeviceId
| where datetime_diff('minute', NetTime, MshtaTime) between (-30 .. 30)
| project MshtaTime, DeviceName, AccountName, MshtaParent, MshtaCmd, SchedCmd, NetApp, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
| sort by MshtaTime desc
ucs.rationale: Confucius reporting (TrendMicro Aug 2021, Uptycs Jan 2021) consistently shows mshta.exe used to stage downloaders that drop Hornbill/Sunbird/ChatSpy/Warzone RAT, immediately backed by a schtasks /create persistence entry pointing into %APPDATA%/%PUBLIC% and HTTP(S) beaconing to attacker web infrastructure registered through commodity providers (T1583.006). Requiring all three signals — mshta-with-
